(o 3) [...] ..., all [...]

(o 4) [...] they rejoiced, they [...]

(o 5) [...] they rejoiced, ... [...]

(o 6) their [...] until [...]

o 77

[...]-⸢li⸣-u₂ ši-mat x [...]

(o 7) [...] ... fate of ... [...]

o 88

[da?]-⸢nun?⸣-ni-tu₂ ša meš-⸢re⸣-[e ...]

(o 8) The Annunitu who(m) wealth [...]

o 99

[...] x-nu-u ša ME ZA x [...]

(o 9) [...] ... of ... [...]

o 1010

[i?-si?]-in-na ša me-lu-lu [...]

(o 10) [...] a festival of playing.

o 1111

[...] ⸢mul⸣-la-a mul-la-a pan-di-šu-un

(o 11) Fill [...], fill them half full!

o 1212

[daš+šur] ⸢d⸣EN.LIL₂!(E₂) ku-ur-ba ⸢iri⸣ŠA₃—IRI

(o 12) [Aššur] (and) Ellil, pray for the Inner City!

o 1313

[...]-⸢mul⸣-li a-na ki-ša₂-di-šu₂-nu

(o 13) [...] ... to their necks.

o 1414

[...] ⸢ik⸣-rib ḪE₂!(I)GAL₂ maš-šur—PAP—AŠ TI.LA ⸢lu⸣-tir

(o 14) [...] prayer of plenty; may Esarhaddon restore life!

o 1515

⸢lib₃⸣-bu ḫa-du-u liš-ša₂-kin

(o 15) May a joyful heart be established!

o 1616

x x [le]-⸢ru⸣-bu ḫa-du-u ka-a-a-nu

(o 16) May constant joy enter [...]!

o 1717

⸢I₃⸣nu ina gul-gul-le-e tab-bi-⸢ka⸣

(o 17) Pour oil from skull-shaped vessels!

o 1818

SIG₂-MEŠ a-na la!(ŠA) ⸢ma⸣-ne₂-e šu-⸢bi⸣-[ša]

(o 18) Gather countless amounts of wool!

o 1919

NINDA-MEŠ ru-⸢ba⸣-a KAŠ-MEŠ ṭu-ḫi-da

(o 19) Increase the bread (and) supply beer copiously!

o 2020

i-na lib₃-bi-⸢šu⸣-nu i-šar-tu₂ eš-ra-a-ni

(o 20) Arrange prosperity among them!

o 2121

KUR.KUR-MEŠ i-⸢riš⸣-ša₂ i-na iriŠA₃—IRI

(o 21) The lands rejoiced in the Inner City and the Inner City ....

o 2222

⸢u₃⸣ iri⸢ŠA₃⸣—IRI i-⸢ša₂⸣-KAK AD ME AN

o 2323

daš+šur dBAD gi-nu-u

(o 23) Aššur (and) Ellil - regular contributions, regular contributions are your reconciliation.

o 2424

gi-⸢nu⸣-u₂ sa-li-ma-⸢ku-nu⸣

o 2525

qa-at ⸢ra⸣-[...]-⸢ti?⸣ paṭ-ṭi-ra

(o 25) Release the hand of ... [...] ...; ... [...] ... the weak one into the breath of the gods; may the harvest of the land be good.

o 2626

in-šu x [...] x i-na ⸢TU₁₅⸣ DINGIR-MEŠ

o 2727

BURU₁₄ ⸢ša⸣ [KUR] lu-u SIG₅iq

o 2828

an-tu₄ [...] ⸢kab?⸣-ba-ra

(o 28) [...] the ear of barley [...] very thick.

o 2929

x x [...] ⸢i⸣-kab-bir

(o 29) [...] becomes thick.

o 3030

[...] x-e il-la-ka

(o 30) [...] he comes.

o 3131

[...] x SIG₄

(o 31) [...] ... mudbrick.

o 3232

[...] pa-ni

(o 32) [...] front.

o 3333

[...] x te-pa-ša₂

(o 33) [...] ... you do.

o 3434

[...] x x

(o 34) [...] ....

Reverse
rr NaN  (start of reverse missing)
r 1'r 1'

[...]-MEŠ [...]

(r 1') [...] ... [...].

r 2'2'

[a-na x x] x-MEŠ-⸢šu₂⸣ [i-kar-ra-bu]

(r 2') [They pray for] his [...]s; [they pray for] his lands; they pray [for] his [...], they pray [for] his descendants, they pray [for] the craftsman, they pray for the city elders!

r 3'3'

[a-na KUR].KUR-MEŠ-⸢šu₂⸣ [i-kar-ra-bu]

r 4'4'

[a-na ...]-⸢ME⸣ni-⸢šu₂ i-kar-ra⸣-[bu]

r 5'5'

[a-na] ⸢NUNUZ⸣-ME-⸢šu₂ i⸣-kar-ra-bu

r 6'6'

[a-na] ⸢DUMU⸣ um-[man] i-kar-ra-bu

r 7'7'

a-⸢na lu₂⸣AB.BA ⸢IRI⸣ i-kar-ra-bu

r 8'8'

u₄-mu ⸢li-is⸣-mu ša dNA₃

(r 8') It is the day of the race of Nabu, a day [of] Ayyaru, a favourable month.

r 9'9'

⸢u₄⸣-mu [ša₂] itiGU₄ ITI ⸢DU₁₀.GA⸣

r 10'10'

⸢im-nu⸣-[ku-nu] KAB-ku-nu SILA IRI-šu₂ a-na e-te-qi

(r 10') To [your] right (and) to your left, to cross the streets of his city, to decide the decisions of the city, wherever we enter, there is abundance (and) attractiveness.

r 11'11'

EŠ.BAR ⸢IRI⸣ [a]-⸢na⸣ KU₅si ⸢E₂⸣ ni-⸢ru⸣-bu ḪE₂.NUN ḪI.LI

r 12'12'

SILA ⸢ni⸣-ti-qu i-ri-ia-aš₂ IRI

(r 12') The city rejoices at the road that we cross.

r 13'13'

is-⸢qu⸣ NIDBA-MEŠ NUN.NA lis-⸢ri⸣-qu

(r 13') May they scatter a share and very big food offerings.

r 14'14'

še?-⸢im⸣ MA MA? U

(r 14') [...] barley.

(5 lines blank)
(colophon)
r 15'15'

[GIM] ⸢LIBIR⸣.RA-šu₂ SAR ⸢E₃⸣ SAR m ⸢mu⸣LALin—aš+šur šam-lu₃-u daq-qu

(r 15') Written and checked [according to] its original. Writer: Mutaqqin-Aššur, the junior scribal apprentice,

(3 lines blank)
r 16'16'

[A md]E₂.A—ḪE₂.NU.MU—ŠEŠ ⸢lu₂⸣ṭup-šar-ru iriBAL.TIL⸢ki⸣

(3 lines blank)
r 17'17'

[A m]⸢dša₃⸣-maš₂—šu-⸢mu₂⸣—i₃-⸢di⸣-na šam-lu₃-u šu-bul-⸢tin⸣-bi

(3 lines blank)
r 18'18'

[A] ⸢m⸣dna₅-bu₄-u—ka-ab-tu₄—ŠEŠ-MEŠ-šu₂ lu₂A.BA iri⸢GU₂⸣.[DU₈.Aki]

[son of] Aya-nuhši-uṣur, scribe of Assur,

[son of] Šamaš-šumu-iddina, the young scribal apprentice,

[son] of Nabu-kabti-ahhešu, scribe of Cutha.
